grade-2-intruder-alarm.intro: A Grade 2 intruder alarm is the go-to specification for many shops, offices, workshops and higher-risk homes across the North West. Icomply designs and installs Grade 2 systems to PD 6662 / BS EN 50131, matching detection, control equipment and signalling to how your building is used. From Stockport we survey risk points properly—doors, windows, internal routes and stock areas—then commission the system with clear handover notes so staff know how to set, unset and respond to faults.
grade-2-intruder-alarm.body: Grade 2 means more resilient detection and tamper protection than basic domestic kits, which insurers and landlords often prefer for commercial and multi-unit property. We fit wired or hybrid layouts, app-enabled keypads where useful, and ARC signalling when monitoring is part of the brief. After installation we can schedule routine maintenance so batteries, detectors and signalling stay reliable year-round. Covering Greater Manchester, Cheshire, Lancashire and beyond, we quote fixed scopes once the survey is agreed—no vague day-rates for standard installs.
grade-2-intruder-alarm.meta_desc: Grade 2 intruder alarm install & maintenance across Greater Manchester & the North West. PD 6662 systems from Icomply in Stockport. Fixed quotes.
focus_points: Risk survey and Grade 2 specification to PD 6662 / BS EN 50131
Wired or hybrid detection with clear set/unset training
Optional ARC monitoring and app/keypad user options
Planned maintenance and fault call-outs from Stockport engineers
faq: What does a Grade 2 intruder alarm include?

Typically a graded control panel, suitable detectors for doors, windows and interiors, power supply with battery backup, and user devices such as keypads or fobs. Exact devices follow the survey and any insurer requirements.
Do I need Grade 2 for a small shop or office?
Many commercial sites and some higher-risk homes are best served at Grade 2. We assess stock value, occupancy and access points, then recommend Grade 2 where it is proportionate and insurer-friendly.
Can you upgrade an existing alarm to Grade 2?
Often yes. We inspect cabling, devices and panel capability, then upgrade or replace only what is needed to meet Grade 2 practice—saving cost where the existing infrastructure is sound.
grade-2-intruder-alarm.seo_keywords: Grade 2 intruder alarm, Grade 2 alarm install, PD 6662 Grade 2, commercial intruder alarm Manchester, Grade 2 security Stockport, BS EN 50131, North West alarm install

pd6662-alarm-system.intro: PD 6662 is the UK scheme that aligns intruder and hold-up alarm systems with European grading under BS EN 50131. When you ask for a PD6662 alarm system, you want design, install and documentation that stand up to insurers, police response criteria and professional maintenance. Icomply delivers PD 6662-focused installs and upgrades from our Stockport base, covering Greater Manchester and the wider North West with practical, site-specific engineering rather than catalogue kits alone.
pd6662-alarm-system.body: We start with a survey of building use, entry routes and existing cabling, then specify panel, detection and signalling to the appropriate grade. Commissioning includes walk-tests, power checks and user training, with paperwork your insurer or facilities auditor can follow. Whether you are fitting a new retail unit, refreshing a warehouse system or bringing an older install closer to current PD 6662 practice, we keep the scope transparent and the price fixed after agreement. Ongoing service visits keep detectors clean, batteries healthy and signalling paths proven.
pd6662-alarm-system.meta_desc: PD6662 alarm system design, install & service in Greater Manchester & the North West. Graded BS EN 50131 systems from Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: PD 6662 / BS EN 50131 graded design and commissioning
Insurer-aware specification for commercial and residential sites
Signalling, power and detection proven at handover
Maintenance contracts and reactive support across the North West
faq: What is a PD6662 alarm system?

It is an intruder (and where required hold-up) alarm installed and maintained in line with the UK PD 6662 scheme and the relevant European standards, including graded equipment and proper documentation.
Does PD 6662 guarantee police response?
Response depends on police force policy, confirmation technology and monitoring arrangements. We can design for confirmed activations and ARC links where that forms part of your security plan.
Will you certificate the install?
Yes. Commissioning records, grade information and handover notes are provided as part of a professional PD 6662-aligned installation.
pd6662-alarm-system.seo_keywords: PD6662 alarm system, PD 6662 install, BS EN 50131 alarm, graded intruder alarm North West, PD6662 Manchester, alarm commissioning Stockport

smoke-shaft-aov.intro: Smoke shaft AOV systems clear smoke from stair cores and common routes so residents and firefighters can move more safely when an alarm is raised. Icomply installs, services and commissions smoke shaft automatic opening vents for apartment blocks and mixed-use buildings across Greater Manchester and the North West. Working to BS 9991 and EN 12101 practice, we check actuators, dampers, controls, interfaces with the fire alarm, and the paperwork your freeholder or managing agent needs for audit.
smoke-shaft-aov.body: A typical smoke shaft AOV package includes shaft vents or dampers, roof or façade terminations where specified, control panels, manual call points or override facilities, and battery-backed power. We test cause-and-effect with the fire detection system so shafts open only in the correct fire scenarios. Planned maintenance keeps motors free, seals sound and controls reliable—essential on taller buildings where access is difficult. From Stockport we support new builds, refurbishments and reactive call-outs when a shaft fails to open or close on test.
smoke-shaft-aov.meta_desc: Smoke shaft AOV install, service & commissioning across the North West. BS 9991 / EN 12101 support from Icomply in Stockport & Greater Manchester.
focus_points: Smoke shaft AOV survey, install and cause-and-effect commissioning
Interface with fire alarm and override facilities
Actuator, damper and control panel maintenance
Service certificates and test records for managing agents
faq: How often should smoke shaft AOVs be serviced?
Most blocks follow at least annual professional servicing plus regular functional checks under the building’s fire safety arrangements. We can align visits with your fire alarm PPM.
Can you work on occupied residential blocks?
Yes. We plan access with managing agents, protect common areas and keep residents informed when shaft testing or plant-room work will create noise or temporary isolation.
Do you repair as well as install?
We diagnose failed actuators, power supplies and control faults, supply suitable replacements where available, and re-commission the shaft path after remedial works.
smoke-shaft-aov.seo_keywords: smoke shaft AOV, smoke shaft vent, AOV service North West, BS 9991 AOV, EN 12101, apartment block smoke control Manchester, AOV maintenance Stockport

hmo-fire-alarm-system.intro: Houses in multiple occupation need fire detection that matches layout, escape routes and local authority licensing expectations—not a one-size domestic kit. Icomply designs, installs and maintains HMO fire alarm systems to BS 5839 practice appropriate to the property category, from interlinked detection in smaller HMOs through to more extensive Grade A systems where risk and guidance demand it. Landlords and agents across Stockport, Greater Manchester and the North West use us for surveys, upgrades and clear certification paperwork.
hmo-fire-alarm-system.body: We walk the property, note storeys, kitchen locations, bedroom doors and existing equipment, then specify detection type, sounder coverage and panel arrangements where required. Installation is planned around tenants where possible, with retests and logbook entries after commissioning. When licensing officers or fire risk assessors ask for improvements, we deliver proportionate upgrades rather than overselling equipment. Ongoing servicing keeps systems ready for inspection and, more importantly, ready to warn occupants at night.

Servicing and upgrades for multi-let portfolios

It depends on storeys, occupancy and your fire risk assessment or licensing conditions. We recommend a system category that matches those factors rather than a generic retail package.
Can you certificate for the council or agent?
We issue commissioning and service documentation suitable for licensing files, managing agents and fire risk assessors, with clear device schedules where applicable.
Do you cover small and large HMOs?
Yes—from converted houses to larger multi-let buildings across the North West, including portfolio programmes for agents with several addresses.

commercial-gas-safety-certificate.intro: A commercial gas safety certificate records that gas appliances, flues and pipework under your control have been checked by a Gas Safe engineer to the standard expected for the premises. Icomply provides commercial gas safety inspections and certification for workplaces, kitchens, plant rooms and multi-tenant sites across Greater Manchester and the North West. From Stockport we schedule inspections around trading hours, flag defects clearly, and issue the paperwork facilities teams and landlords need for compliance files.
commercial-gas-safety-certificate.body: Commercial gas safety work goes beyond a quick boiler glance: we assess appliances in service, ventilation and flue arrangements, and visible pipework integrity within the agreed scope. Unsafe situations are explained plainly with recommended remedial actions. Where you manage several units, we can batch visits and keep a simple asset list so renewal dates do not slip. Combined with our wider gas and heating support, you get one local contractor for inspection, remedial works and re-certification after repairs.
commercial-gas-safety-certificate.meta_desc: Commercial gas safety certificate & Gas Safe inspections across Greater Manchester & the North West. Clear reports from Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: Gas Safe commercial inspections for workplaces and kitchens

Certification suited to FM, landlord and audit files
Multi-site scheduling across the North West
faq: How often is a commercial gas safety check required?
Frequency follows legislation, your insurer and any lease or company policy—many businesses inspect annually as a minimum. We can diary renewals so certificates do not lapse.
Is a commercial certificate the same as a landlord CP12?
Residential landlord gas safety records and commercial workplace checks serve related but different contexts. We confirm the correct certificate type for your premises before the visit.
What if appliances fail the inspection?
We explain the issue, isolate or make safe where required under Gas Safe rules, and quote remedial works so you can retest and obtain a clean certificate once fixed.

consumer-unit-upgrade.intro: Upgrading a consumer unit is one of the most effective ways to bring an older electrical installation closer to modern BS 7671 expectations—especially where residual current protection is missing or the board is overloaded. Icomply plans and installs consumer unit upgrades for homes, landlords and light commercial premises across Stockport, Greater Manchester and the North West. We assess existing circuits, surge and RCD/RCBO strategy, then swap boards with minimal disruption and full certification on completion.
consumer-unit-upgrade.body: A good upgrade is more than a metal box change: circuit labelling, earthing and bonding checks, and correct protective device selection all matter. Where cables or circuits are not fit for a new board, we explain options before work starts so you are not surprised mid-job. Metal dual-RCD or all-RCBO boards are specified to suit the property and your budget. After energising we test, issue an Electrical Installation Certificate for the work, and leave a tidy cupboard you can understand at a glance.
consumer-unit-upgrade.meta_desc: Consumer unit upgrade to BS 7671 across Greater Manchester & the North West. RCD/RCBO boards, testing & certificates from Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: Survey of circuits, earthing and board location
RCD or all-RCBO consumer unit options to BS 7671
Labelling, testing and Electrical Installation Certificate
Landlord and homeowner upgrades with fixed agreed pricing
faq: How long does a consumer unit upgrade take?
Many domestic swaps complete in a day if circuits are healthy. Older or complex installs may need extra time—we confirm after survey.
Will I need a full rewire as well?
Not always. The upgrade can proceed if circuits are satisfactory; we only recommend rewires or partial rewires where testing shows genuine defects.
Do you notify Building Control?
Notifiable domestic work is handled under the appropriate Part P route by our qualified electricians, with certificates for your records and solicitor packs.
consumer-unit-upgrade.seo_keywords: consumer unit upgrade, fuse board upgrade, RCBO consumer unit, BS 7671 board change, consumer unit Manchester, electrical board Stockport

eicr-report.intro: An EICR report—Electrical Installation Condition Report—sets out the condition of fixed wiring after inspection and testing to BS 7671. Icomply produces clear, actionable EICR reports for landlords, agents, homeowners and commercial clients across the North West. Our Stockport-based electricians do not hide behind jargon: codes are explained, photos or notes highlight issues where helpful, and remedial priorities are easy to follow.
eicr-report.body: The visit covers visual inspection plus dead and live testing of circuits within the agreed scope, from consumer unit to final outlets and fixed equipment connections. You receive a formal EICR with observations coded C1, C2, C3 or FI as applicable, plus an overall satisfactory or unsatisfactory outcome. Unsatisfactory reports come with a practical remedial path so you can fix issues and, where needed, obtain confirmation that dangerous or potentially dangerous faults have been addressed. Portfolio clients can book recurring cycles so reports stay current for tenancy and compliance diaries.
eicr-report.meta_desc: EICR report by qualified electricians in Greater Manchester & the North West. Clear BS 7671 condition reports from Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: Full inspection and testing to produce a formal EICR
Plain-English explanation of C1, C2, C3 and FI codes
Remedial quotes aligned to report findings
Landlord, HMO and commercial reporting cycles
faq: What is the difference between an EICR report and a certificate?
People often use the terms interchangeably. The EICR is the condition report document itself; it is sometimes called an EICR certificate in everyday speech.
How long is an EICR report valid?
The inspector recommends the next inspection interval based on condition and use—commonly five years for many rentals, sooner if the installation is poor or high-risk.
Will you fix faults found on the report?
Yes. We can carry out electrical remedial works and, where appropriate, issue further certification so your file shows both the original EICR and completed repairs.
eicr-report.seo_keywords: EICR report, electrical installation condition report, EICR Manchester, landlord EICR report, BS 7671 inspection Stockport, unsatisfactory EICR remedials

eicr-certificate.intro: Searching for an EICR certificate usually means you need formal proof that fixed electrics have been inspected and tested. Icomply issues Electrical Installation Condition Reports—widely referred to as EICR certificates—for private rentals, HMOs, offices and other premises across Greater Manchester and the North West. From Stockport we book efficient visits, test to BS 7671, and deliver documentation suitable for agents, tenants and compliance folders.
eicr-certificate.body: Our electricians isolate and test circuits safely, check protective devices, and assess wear, damage and earthing arrangements. The completed EICR certificate pack states whether the installation is satisfactory, lists coded observations, and recommends when the next inspection should take place. If the outcome is unsatisfactory, we prioritise dangerous faults and provide a remedial programme rather than leaving you with a document you cannot act on. Digital and hard copies can be supplied to match how your agency stores records.
eicr-certificate.meta_desc: EICR certificate for landlords & businesses across Greater Manchester & the North West. BS 7671 testing from Icomply Stockport. Book online.
focus_points: BS 7671 EICR (condition report) for homes and commercial sites
Satisfactory / unsatisfactory outcomes with coded observations
Agent-friendly documentation and renewal reminders
Linked remedial works from the same electrical team
faq: Is an EICR the same as a Part P certificate?
No. An EICR assesses the condition of an existing installation. New or altered circuits usually need an Electrical Installation Certificate under different rules.
How soon can I get an EICR certificate?
We aim for same-week appointments where capacity and access allow, with the report following promptly after testing is complete.
Do tenants need to be present?
Access to the consumer unit and all rooms with electrical outlets is required. Agents often arrange keys; we confirm access arrangements when booking.
eicr-certificate.seo_keywords: EICR certificate, electrical safety certificate, EICR for rental, EICR North West, electrical certificate Manchester, landlord EICR Stockport

landlord-eicr.intro: Landlord EICR duties mean private rented properties in England generally need a valid Electrical Installation Condition Report, with copies available to tenants and local authorities on request. Icomply specialises in landlord EICR testing across Stockport, Greater Manchester and the wider North West—efficient access arrangements, clear reports, and remedial support so lettings are not left in limbo after an unsatisfactory result.
landlord-eicr.body: We work daily with landlords and letting agents: void-period testing, mid-tenancy access where agreed, and portfolio schedules that keep multiple addresses on a rolling plan. Reports highlight what must be fixed before you can confidently re-let or demonstrate compliance. When C1 or C2 codes appear, our electricians can quote and complete remedials, then provide the paperwork trail agents expect. Combine EICR visits with smoke alarm checks or consumer unit upgrades when you want one coordinated compliance package.
landlord-eicr.meta_desc: Landlord EICR testing & reports across Greater Manchester & the North West. Agent-friendly BS 7671 inspections from Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: Private rental EICRs aligned to landlord electrical safety rules
Agent and portfolio booking with flexible access
Plain-English codes and prioritised remedials
Supporting certificates after fixed wiring repairs
faq: How often do landlords need an EICR?
For most private rentals in England, at least every five years or at a change of tenancy if the existing report has expired—unless the previous report recommends a shorter interval.
What if my landlord EICR is unsatisfactory?
You should arrange remedial works for dangerous and potentially dangerous faults within the expected timescales. We can complete those works and document completion.
Can you email the report to my agent?
Yes. We supply digital copies to landlords and nominated agents so tenancy files stay complete.
landlord-eicr.seo_keywords: landlord EICR, rental property EICR, landlord electrical certificate, private rented EICR Manchester, letting agent EICR Stockport, North West landlord testing

commercial-eicr.intro: A commercial EICR assesses fixed electrical installations in offices, retail units, warehouses, hospitality venues and other workplaces. Icomply delivers commercial EICR programmes that respect trading hours, multi-tenant access and facilities management processes. Based in Stockport, our electricians cover Greater Manchester and the North West with methodical BS 7671 testing and reports written for duty holders, not just electricians.
commercial-eicr.body: Commercial sites often have three-phase boards, distribution circuits, emergency lighting interfaces and higher load equipment. We plan isolation windows with your team, label findings against distribution boards, and code observations so capital works can be budgeted sensibly. Unsatisfactory outcomes are paired with remedial options—from simple device changes to phased board upgrades. For multi-site estates we standardise report formats and inspection intervals so head office compliance dashboards stay consistent.

Three-phase and distribution board experience
Duty-holder friendly reporting and remedial roadmaps
Multi-site and FM contract testing programmes
faq: How often should a commercial EICR be done?

Intervals depend on environment, usage and previous condition—commonly three to five years for many workplaces, more frequent in harsh or high-risk settings. We recommend a suitable period on each report.
Will testing disrupt my business?
Some circuits need isolation. We schedule around trading peaks where possible and agree which areas can be tested out of hours.
Can you test only part of a large building?
Yes. Phased or zoned EICRs are common on large estates; we define scope clearly so insurers and auditors understand what has been covered.

eicr-cost.intro: EICR cost depends on property size, number of circuits, access and whether the installation is domestic, HMO or commercial. Icomply gives straightforward EICR pricing guidance and fixed quotes after we know bedrooms, consumer unit layout or floor area—so landlords and businesses across Greater Manchester and the North West can budget without open-ended day rates. From Stockport we keep fees competitive while still allowing proper testing time; rushed cheap tests often miss faults that cost more later.
eicr-cost.body: A one-bedroom flat with a modern board costs less than a large HMO or a multi-board commercial unit. Factors that influence EICR cost include storeys, outbuildings, difficulty of isolation, and how much remedial work might be expected. We quote the inspection clearly, then price remedials separately against coded findings so you are not paying for repairs you may not need. Ask about portfolio rates if you manage several rentals—batching visits reduces travel and admin cost per address.
eicr-cost.meta_desc: EICR cost explained for landlords & businesses in Greater Manchester & the North West. Fixed-price quotes from Icomply Stockport—no vague day rates.
focus_points: Transparent EICR pricing by property type and circuit count
Fixed quotes once access and size are confirmed
Remedials priced separately against report codes
Portfolio discounts for multi-property landlords and agents
faq: Why do EICR prices vary so much online?
Ultra-low prices may limit testing time or exclude larger properties. A proper EICR needs safe isolation, full circuit tests and a thorough report—we price for that standard.
Is the certificate included in the EICR cost?
Yes. Our quoted inspection fee includes the formal Electrical Installation Condition Report for the agreed scope.
Do you charge extra for evenings or weekends?
Out-of-hours commercial testing can attract a premium when isolation must happen outside normal hours—we confirm any uplift before booking.
eicr-cost.seo_keywords: EICR cost, EICR price, how much is an EICR, landlord EICR cost Manchester, electrical certificate cost Stockport, commercial EICR price North West

eicr-testing.intro: EICR testing is the hands-on inspection and measurement process behind every Electrical Installation Condition Report. Icomply electricians carry out EICR testing to BS 7671 for homes, rentals and commercial premises throughout Greater Manchester and the North West. From our Stockport base we bring calibrated test instruments, methodical circuit-by-circuit procedures, and the discipline to record results accurately—not just tick a form.
eicr-testing.body: Testing typically includes continuity, insulation resistance, polarity, earth fault loop impedance, RCD performance and functional checks, alongside a structured visual inspection. We identify overloaded boards, damaged accessories, poor bonding and ageing cables that visual checks alone might miss. Results feed directly into coded observations on your EICR. Where access is restricted or circuits cannot be fully isolated, we note limitations honestly rather than inventing complete results. Book EICR testing as a one-off or as a recurring compliance service for your portfolio.
eicr-testing.meta_desc: EICR testing to BS 7671 across Greater Manchester & the North West. Thorough inspection & condition reports from Icomply Stockport electricians.
focus_points: Instrument testing of fixed wiring to BS 7671 methods
Visual inspection plus measured results on every report
Honest limitation notes where access prevents full tests
Follow-on remedials by the same local team
faq: How long does EICR testing take?
A typical flat may take a few hours; larger houses and commercial units take longer. Circuit count and accessibility are the main drivers.
Will power be off during testing?
Yes for periods on each circuit. We try to stage isolation so essential areas are not offline longer than necessary.
What happens after testing?
You receive the EICR with codes and recommendations. If works are needed, we quote remedials and retest affected circuits as required.
eicr-testing.seo_keywords: EICR testing, electrical testing BS 7671, condition report testing, EICR electrician Manchester, fixed wire testing Stockport, North West electrical inspection

Icomply provides periodic electrical inspection for landlords, facilities managers and homeowners who need formal proof that fixed wiring remains safe for continued use. Covering Stockport, Greater Manchester and the wider North West, we combine thorough testing with practical advice on when to repair, upgrade or simply re-inspect at the next interval.
periodic-electrical-inspection.body: Periodic inspection is not the same as PAT testing of appliances or a quick visual look around a board. We examine distribution equipment, wiring systems, accessories and earthing arrangements, then test circuits systematically. Findings are coded so you can prioritise safety-critical items. Many clients align periodic electrical inspection with insurance renewals, lease events or five-year landlord cycles. We diary next due dates and can coordinate related works such as emergency lighting tests or consumer unit upgrades during the same visit window where useful.

faq: Is periodic inspection the same as an EICR?
Yes in modern practice—the Electrical Installation Condition Report is the document produced after periodic inspection and testing of an existing installation.
Can you inspect only the landlord’s supply in a block?
Scope can be limited to landlord areas if defined clearly. Tenant demise circuits may need separate access and agreements.
Do you provide a schedule of test results?
Reports include the formal condition assessment and supporting test data as required for a complete EICR package.

fuse-board-replacement.intro: Fuse board replacement—now more accurately a consumer unit change—updates outdated rewireable fuses or early MCBs to modern protective devices with proper residual current protection. Icomply replaces fuse boards in homes, HMOs and light commercial premises across the North West, with BS 7671 design choices, neat labelling and full certification. Based in Stockport, we explain whether a dual-RCD or all-RCBO board is the better fit before tools come out.
fuse-board-replacement.body: Old fuse boards can lack adequate earth leakage protection, spare ways and clear identification of circuits. Replacement improves safety for sockets, bathrooms and outdoor circuits and often resolves nuisance issues when circuits are rebalanced. We check main earthing, bonding and cable condition so the new board is not fitted onto an unsafe foundation. After installation we test, certificate and walk you through the new layout. If your insurer or buyer’s survey flagged the board, we can prioritise appointments to keep sales and remortgages moving.
fuse-board-replacement.meta_desc: Fuse board replacement & modern consumer units across Greater Manchester & the North West. Certified upgrades from Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: Replacement of outdated fuse boards with modern consumer units
RCD/RCBO protection strategy tailored to the property
Earthing, bonding and circuit checks before energising
Electrical Installation Certificate on completion
faq: Can you reuse my existing cables?
Often yes if they are in good condition and correctly sized. Damaged or undersized tails and circuits are upgraded as needed and agreed first.
Is a metal consumer unit required?
Current practice for domestic installations generally uses non-combustible enclosures meeting BS 7671 expectations—we specify compliant boards as standard.
Will the whole house power be off?
Yes during the changeover. We plan the outage window with you and restore supply as soon as testing allows.
fuse-board-replacement.seo_keywords: fuse board replacement, replace fuse box, consumer unit replacement, modern fuse board Manchester, electrical board change Stockport, North West electrician

partial-rewire.intro: A partial rewire renews selected circuits or areas—kitchens, bathrooms, extensions or deteriorated loft runs—without stripping every wall in the building. Icomply plans partial rewires where testing shows localised risk or a refurbishment creates new demand, working to BS 7671 across Greater Manchester and the North West. From Stockport we survey first, map what stays and what goes, and quote a defined scope so you are not sold a full rewire you do not need.
partial-rewire.body: Partial rewires suit properties with mixed-age wiring: sound lighting circuits upstairs, for example, but unsafe socket circuits in a wet room. We isolate works carefully, update the consumer unit when protection needs changing, and certificate the altered circuits properly. Coordination with plasterers and kitchen fitters keeps first and second fixes on programme. After completion you receive installation certification for the new work plus advice on when a full EICR or further stages make sense. Ideal for landlords refreshing voids and homeowners renovating room by room.
partial-rewire.meta_desc: Partial rewire for kitchens, bathrooms & problem circuits across Greater Manchester & the North West. BS 7671 work by Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: Targeted rewiring based on survey and test results
Kitchen, bathroom, extension and void refurb packages
Consumer unit updates where protection must match new circuits
Clear certification for altered installations only
faq: When is a partial rewire better than a full rewire?
When inspection shows most circuits are satisfactory and only specific areas fail or are being rebuilt. We only recommend partial work when it is electrically coherent and safe.
Will you chase walls?
Where new cable routes need concealment we chase or use agreed surface containment—finishers usually make good decorations unless included in the quote.
Can you stage a partial rewire over several visits?
Yes. Room-by-room programmes are common on occupied homes; we keep temporary safe supplies where practical between stages.
partial-rewire.seo_keywords: partial rewire, kitchen rewire, bathroom electrical rewire, partial house rewire Manchester, electrical renovation Stockport, BS 7671 rewire North West

portable-appliance-testing.intro: Portable appliance testing (PAT) checks plug-in electrical equipment used at work—and often in HMOs and managed housing—so damaged leads, failed insulation and incorrect fuses are caught before they cause shocks or fires. Icomply delivers practical PAT programmes for offices, workshops, hospitality and residential portfolios across the North West. From Stockport we label appliances, record results and focus effort on higher-risk kit rather than ritual testing of everything every month without reason.
portable-appliance-testing.body: Good PAT combines formal inspection with electrical tests suited to Class I and Class II equipment, plus clear fail tags when items must be removed from service. We help you set sensible retest intervals based on environment and usage, which keeps costs under control while meeting HSE expectations for maintenance of work equipment. Combined visits with fixed-wire EICRs or emergency lighting tests reduce contractor traffic on site. Digital asset lists make it easier for managers to track hundreds of appliances across multiple floors or addresses.
portable-appliance-testing.meta_desc: Portable appliance testing (PAT) for workplaces & portfolios across Greater Manchester & the North West. Clear labels & records from Icomply.
focus_points: On-site PAT inspection and testing with pass/fail labelling
Risk-based intervals for offices, workshops and HMOs
Asset lists and certificates for audit and insurance files
Combined compliance visits with other electrical services
faq: Is PAT testing a legal annual requirement?
The law requires work equipment to be maintained safely; PAT is a widely accepted method. Frequency should match risk—not an arbitrary one-size rule for every item.
Do you test IT equipment and extension leads?
Yes. Computers, monitors, leads and multi-way blocks are common inclusions; we can exclude user-owned devices if your policy requires.
What happens to failed appliances?
They are failed-labelled and should be removed from service until repaired or replaced. We can advise on common plug and lead repairs where appropriate.
portable-appliance-testing.seo_keywords: portable appliance testing, PAT testing, PAT test Manchester, workplace PAT Stockport, appliance testing North West, HMO PAT testing

home-ev-charger.intro: A home EV charger turns a driveway or garage into a reliable overnight charging bay with safer, faster top-ups than a three-pin trickle lead. Icomply designs and installs domestic EV charge points across Stockport, Greater Manchester and the North West, matching charger rating to your supply capacity, earthing arrangement and parking layout. We handle isolation, protective devices, cable routes and certification so your installation is tidy, compliant and ready for daily use.
home-ev-charger.body: Survey matters: not every fuse board or PME earthing arrangement suits every charger without additional protection measures. faq: What charge rate can I have at home?
Many homes suit a 7 kW single-phase charger if the main fuse and board capacity allow. We confirm after assessing diversity and existing loads.
Do I need a consumer unit upgrade first?
Sometimes. Limited spare ways or lack of suitable RCD protection can mean a board upgrade before or during charger installation—we flag this at survey.
Can you install on a driveway pillar or garage?
Yes, subject to cable route, weatherproof mounting and safe isolation. We discuss civil works if a new post or groundworks are needed.

High-load equipment, large kitchens, workshops and multi-charger EV sites often need it. We assess nameplate loads and diversity to confirm.
Can you convert a single-phase building to three-phase?

emergency-electrician.intro: When power fails, a board burns out or a dangerous fault appears, you need an emergency electrician who answers the phone and turns up with the right test gear. Icomply provides reactive electrical call-outs across Stockport, Greater Manchester and the wider North West for landlords, businesses and homeowners. We prioritise safety—isolating hazards, restoring essential circuits where possible, and explaining permanent repairs with clear pricing before major works begin.
emergency-electrician.body: Emergency attendance covers no-power situations, RCD trips that will not reset, damaged consumer units, smoke from accessories, and urgent tenancy issues that cannot wait for a routine slot. We carry common protective devices and accessories to restore supply quickly when the fault is localised. If the installation needs deeper investigation or a partial rewire, we make safe, document findings and schedule follow-up. Outside peak hours availability varies; call early with your postcode and symptoms so we can triage severity and ETA honestly.
emergency-electrician.meta_desc: Emergency electrician across Greater Manchester & the North West. Reactive electrical faults, isolations & repairs from Icomply Stockport.

Follow-up permanent repairs with fixed quotes
faq: Do you offer 24/7 emergency cover?
We provide reactive attendance as capacity allows and prioritise genuine safety risks. Confirm current out-of-hours options when you call.
What should I do before the electrician arrives?
If safe, switch off at the consumer unit, keep clear of burning smells or exposed wires, and note which circuits failed. Never touch water-damaged electrics.
Will I get a certificate for emergency repairs?
Notifiable or significant alterations are certified. Pure make-safe isolations are documented; full certificates follow when permanent remedial works are completed.
emergency-electrician.seo_keywords: emergency electrician, 24 hour electrician, emergency electrical repair Manchester, no power electrician Stockport, reactive electrician North West

rcbo-consumer-unit.intro: An RCBO consumer unit gives each circuit combined overcurrent and residual current protection, limiting the inconvenience of a single RCD taking out half the house when one appliance faults. Icomply supplies and installs RCBO consumer units for homes, HMOs and light commercial sites across Greater Manchester and the North West. From Stockport we design board layouts, label every way clearly, and certificate the upgrade to BS 7671.
rcbo-consumer-unit.body: All-RCBO boards are popular for larger properties and rentals where nuisance tripping causes tenant complaints. We select RCBO ratings to match cable sizes and loads, consider surge protection devices where appropriate, and check main earth and bonding before energising. Existing circuits are dressed neatly into the new enclosure with spare ways for future EV or kitchen circuits where space allows. After testing you will know exactly which RCBO protects which room—useful for any future fault finding.
rcbo-consumer-unit.meta_desc: RCBO consumer unit install across Greater Manchester & the North West. All-RCBO boards, testing & certificates from Icomply Stockport.

Full BS 7671 testing and Electrical Installation Certificate
faq: Is an RCBO board better than dual RCD?
RCBOs isolate faults to a single circuit, which many households prefer. Dual-RCD boards can still be compliant; we explain trade-offs on survey.
Are RCBO consumer units more expensive?
Device cost is higher than shared RCDs, but many clients value the resilience—especially in rentals and larger homes.
Can RCBOs be added to my existing board?
Sometimes individual ways can be upgraded if the board accepts RCBOs; often a full board replacement is cleaner and more reliable.

bs-7671-inspection.intro: A BS 7671 inspection assesses electrical installations against the IET Wiring Regulations—the national standard for electrical safety in the UK. Icomply carries out BS 7671 inspection and testing for condition reports, verification of new work and compliance audits across Stockport, Greater Manchester and the North West. Does a BS 7671 inspection include appliances?

We design circuit allocation for kitchens, EV readiness, data co-location and high-spec lighting where the brief demands it. Snagging is handled promptly so completions are not delayed. Full Electrical Installation Certificates and schedules form part of the owner’s manual pack. Whether a single self-build or a small site of houses, clear drawings and labelled boards make future maintenance simpler for residents.
new-build-electrical-installation.meta_desc: New build electrical installation across Greater Manchester & the North West. First & second fix, testing & certificates from Icomply.
focus_points: First and second fix electrical packages for new builds
Consumer units, lighting, power and detection circuits
Programme coordination with builders and other trades
Full BS 7671 certification for handover packs
faq: Do you work from architects’ drawings?
Yes. We review electrical layouts, suggest practical amendments where needed, and install to the agreed issue of drawings.
Can you include data and TV points?
We install containment and outlets as specified; active networking equipment is usually by others unless agreed.
When is testing done?
After second fix and before practical completion, with any snag retests before final certificate issue.
new-build-electrical-installation.seo_keywords: new build electrical installation, first fix electrics, second fix electrician, new build wiring Manchester, self build electrician Stockport

electrical-board-upgrade.intro: An electrical board upgrade modernises the heart of the installation—the consumer unit or distribution board—improving protection, capacity and clarity of circuit identification. Icomply delivers electrical board upgrades for domestic, landlord and commercial clients across Greater Manchester and the North West. From Stockport we assess spare ways, RCD strategy, surge protection and cable condition, then upgrade with minimal downtime and full certification.
electrical-board-upgrade.body: Board upgrades are often triggered by EICR codes, homebuyer reports, EV charger plans or repeated nuisance tripping. We specify dual-RCD or RCBO configurations, metal enclosures where required, and tidy dressing of tails and circuit cables. Isolation is planned so freezers and home workers know the outage window. After energising, protective devices are tested and circuits labelled in plain language. The result is a safer, more maintainable installation ready for future circuits and inspections.
electrical-board-upgrade.meta_desc: Electrical board upgrade to modern BS 7671 protection across Greater Manchester & the North West. Icomply Stockport fixed quotes.
focus_points: Consumer unit and distribution board upgrades
RCD/RCBO and surge protection options
Clear circuit labelling and testing on completion
Domestic, HMO and light commercial experience
faq: Is a board upgrade the same as a rewire?
No. The board can often be upgraded while existing circuits remain if they test satisfactorily. Rewires address the cables themselves.
Will my insurance company accept the upgrade?
A certified upgrade addressing safety shortfalls is generally viewed positively; supply your Electrical Installation Certificate when asked.
How long is the power off?
Most domestic board upgrades need a day of interruption; we confirm timing once circuit count and complexity are known.
electrical-board-upgrade.seo_keywords: electrical board upgrade, consumer unit upgrade, distribution board upgrade, fuse board upgrade Manchester, electrical board Stockport

distribution-board-installation.intro: Distribution board installation provides organised, protected power splitting for commercial units, landlord supplies, plant rooms and larger homes with sub-mains. Icomply designs and installs distribution boards to BS 7671 across the North West, with correct device selection, discrimination and labelling. Based in Stockport, we handle single and three-phase boards, metal enclosures, and interfaces to meters, isolators and generators where specified.
distribution-board-installation.body: A well-installed distribution board is safe to operate and easy to maintain: clear schedules, logical way allocation, adequate spare capacity and tidy cable entry. We calculate loads, select breakers and RCDs/RCBOs, and verify earthing arrangements before energising. New boards for fit-outs, temporary builders’ supplies upgrades, and permanent plant feeds are all within scope. Testing includes insulation, polarity, loop impedance and functional checks of protective devices. Handover includes as-fitted schedules your FM team can actually use during future isolations.
distribution-board-installation.meta_desc: Distribution board installation across Greater Manchester & the North West. Single & three-phase boards by Icomply Stockport electricians.
focus_points: New distribution board design and installation
Single-phase and three-phase commercial boards
Labelling, spare capacity and maintenance access
Full testing and BS 7671 certification
faq: What is the difference between a consumer unit and a distribution board?
Consumer unit usually refers to domestic boards; distribution board is the broader term for commercial and sub-main boards—though language overlaps in practice.
Can you replace a board without rewiring the whole building?
Yes if final circuits and sub-mains are sound. We test and only extend scope where defects require it.
Do you install metered landlord boards?
We install landlord distribution and can coordinate with meter operators; metering hardware supply depends on the energy provider arrangement.
distribution-board-installation.seo_keywords: distribution board installation, DB install, commercial distribution board, three phase distribution board Manchester, electrical DB Stockport

emergency-lighting-electrical-works.intro: Emergency lighting electrical works keep escape routes lit when mains power fails—mandatory for most commercial premises and many residential blocks. Icomply installs, converts, tests and certificates emergency lighting as electrical works under BS 5266 across Greater Manchester and the North West. From Stockport we handle new luminaire circuits, central battery interfaces where specified, self-test systems and the monthly/annual testing regimes facilities managers rely on.
emergency-lighting-electrical-works.body: Effective emergency lighting needs correct mounting heights, spacing, pictogram exit signage and verified duration. Our electrical works cover supply, containment, switching arrangements that do not defeat emergency function, and integration with fire strategies where required. We replace failed batteries and modules, upgrade fluorescent conversions to LED emergency packs, and document results in logbooks. Combined visits with fire alarm or EICR work reduce site disruption. Whether a small shop or a multi-stair apartment core, we design to the risk profile rather than a generic parts list.
emergency-lighting-electrical-works.meta_desc: Emergency lighting electrical works across Greater Manchester & the North West. BS 5266 install, test & repair from Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: Emergency lighting install and circuit alterations
BS 5266 testing, duration checks and logbooks
LED conversions, batteries and self-test options
Commercial and residential block coverage
faq: How often must emergency lighting be tested?
Typically monthly functional tests and annual full-duration tests, with records kept—exact duties follow your fire risk assessment and guidance.
Can you add emergency packs to existing lights?
Often yes if luminaires are suitable; otherwise dedicated emergency luminaires are installed. We advise on the cleanest compliant option.
Do you certificate emergency lighting works?
Yes. Installation and periodic test records are provided so your compliance folder stays complete.
emergency-lighting-electrical-works.seo_keywords: emergency lighting electrical works, emergency lighting install, BS 5266 testing, emergency light electrician Manchester, emergency lighting Stockport

landlord-electrical-certificate-north-we.intro: Looking for a landlord electrical certificate in the North West? Icomply issues EICRs and related installation certificates for rental properties across Greater Manchester, Lancashire, Cheshire, Merseyside and into Cumbria. Based in Stockport (SK2), we understand regional agent practices, HMO licensing variations and the need for prompt PDF reports. One team covers inspection, remedials and re-certification so multi-county portfolios are not juggling several electricians.
landlord-electrical-certificate-north-we.body: North West landlords manage everything from terrace flats in Manchester to coastal HMOs and rural lets—each with different access challenges. We schedule efficiently by postcode cluster, keep agents updated, and price fairly whether you have one flat or fifty. Certificates meet BS 7671 expectations and are suitable for tenancy files and local authority requests. When reports are unsatisfactory, local engineers return to complete remedials without you starting again with a new contractor. Request portfolio rates and preferred-supplier terms for high-volume agencies.
landlord-electrical-certificate-north-we.meta_desc: Landlord electrical certificate North West—EICR for rentals across Greater Manchester, Lancashire & Cheshire. Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: Landlord EICR certificates across the wider North West
Stockport base with multi-county coverage
Agent PDF delivery and portfolio scheduling
Remedials and follow-up certification from one contractor
faq: Which North West areas do you cover?
Greater Manchester, Lancashire, Cheshire, Merseyside and Cumbria are within our regular coverage from Stockport—confirm your postcode when booking.
Can one certificate cover a whole HMO?
One EICR can cover the installation in a single HMO address when fully accessed; large buildings may need clearly defined scopes.
Do you work with online landlords outside the region?
Yes, if the property is in our service area we can deal with remote landlords and local agents for keys and reports.
landlord-electrical-certificate-north-we.seo_keywords: landlord electrical certificate North West, North West EICR, landlord certificate Greater Manchester, rental EICR Lancashire, Cheshire landlord electrician, Stockport EICR

fire-alarm-certificate.intro: A fire alarm certificate records that a system has been installed, commissioned or serviced to an agreed standard—typically with BS 5839 in mind for non-domestic and many multi-occupancy buildings. Icomply issues clear fire alarm certification after installation, modification and routine maintenance across Greater Manchester and the North West. From Stockport our engineers leave logbook entries, device counts and cause-and-effect notes that facilities managers and freeholders can show insurers and fire risk assessors.
fire-alarm-certificate.body: Certification without meaningful testing is worthless. We exercise detectors, call points, sounders, power supplies and signalling paths within the service scope, then document pass/fail results and defects. New installs receive commissioning packs; existing systems receive service certificates aligned to the visit type. When faults need parts, we quote remedials and retest before closing the job. Combine fire alarm certification with emergency lighting tests when you want one compliance attendance for the building.
fire-alarm-certificate.meta_desc: Fire alarm certificate for install & service across Greater Manchester & the North West. BS 5839 documentation from Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: Commissioning and service certificates for fire alarms
BS 5839-aligned testing and logbook completion
Defect reporting with remedial follow-up
Commercial, HMO and residential block coverage
faq: Is a fire alarm certificate the same as a fire risk assessment?
No. The FRA is a wider building safety assessment. Our certificate evidences work on the fire detection and alarm system itself.
How often should systems be certificated through service?
Many BS 5839 systems are serviced at least every six months by a competent person, with user tests more frequently—confirm against your FRA and insurer.
Can you certificate a system you did not install?
Yes. We service and certificate existing systems after inspection; major unknowns may need investigation before we sign a clean service record.
fire-alarm-certificate.seo_keywords: fire alarm certificate, fire alarm service certificate, BS 5839 certificate, fire alarm commissioning Manchester, fire alarm paperwork Stockport

bs-5839-fire-alarm.intro: BS 5839 is the British Standard series that guides design, installation, commissioning and maintenance of fire detection and fire alarm systems in non-domestic premises—and informs many residential and HMO specifications. Icomply delivers BS 5839 fire alarm design, install, service and upgrade work across the North West. From Stockport we match system category and device coverage to building use, escape strategy and your fire risk assessment, then commission with auditable cause-and-effect.
bs-5839-fire-alarm.body: Whether you need a manual system for a small workshop or automatic detection throughout a multi-storey block, BS 5839 provides the framework for device siting, sound pressure levels, power supplies and zoning. We install conventional and addressable systems, interface with AOVs and door release where required, and train responsible persons on weekly tests. Maintenance visits keep false alarms down and reliability up. Documentation includes certificates, zone charts and logbook setup so the system remains manageable long after handover.
bs-5839-fire-alarm.meta_desc: BS 5839 fire alarm design, install & service across Greater Manchester & the North West. Competent engineers from Icomply Stockport.
focus_points: BS 5839 category selection and system design
Conventional and addressable install with commissioning
Interfaces to AOV, door release and monitoring
Planned maintenance, logbooks and user training
faq: Which BS 5839 category do I need?
It depends on premises type and fire risk assessment—for example L1–L5 for automatic life protection variants or M for manual systems. We recommend a category after survey.
Do you upgrade non-compliant older systems?
Yes. We assess gaps against current practice and deliver proportionate upgrades rather than unnecessary full rip-outs where parts of the system remain sound.
Can BS 5839 systems link to smoke control?
Cause-and-effect can trigger AOVs and other plant. We coordinate interfaces carefully so spurious signals do not open vents without a genuine fire signal.
bs-5839-fire-alarm.seo_keywords: BS 5839 fire alarm, BS 5839 install, fire detection BS 5839, addressable fire alarm Manchester, BS 5839 maintenance Stockport, North West fire alarm
